tasks.title: Fix Phase 3 completion race: ensure terminal archive + completion artifact despite dirty repo
evidence: User report: Phase 3 completion intermittently fails with supervisor error claiming task not archived even though status is done and moved to done.json.
User report: supervisor also reports missing completion artifact in .ralph/cache/completions for the task ID.
User report: error mentions dirty repo and lists modified queue/done.json alongside other files, suggesting ordering or race in Phase 3 checks.
plan: Locate Phase 3 completion flow and its checks for archive status, completion artifact, and dirty repo state.
Reproduce or simulate the reported sequence to understand why the archive check fails when done.json is updated.
Fix ordering/race so terminal archive status is verified after persisting queue/done changes, and ensure completion artifacts are created reliably.
Clarify dirty-repo handling to avoid misclassifying queue/done updates as incomplete completion (or explicitly document/handle this case).
Add tests for success and failure modes, including dirty repo with updated queue/done.json and missing completion artifact.
Update CLI docs/help if Phase 3 flow or requirements change.
Run `make ci`.
tasks.request:
There is a bug and/or race condition intermittently happening upon phase 3 agent completion which causes the superviosr to engage and interrupt and nothing can be done to get it to finish properly and cleanly. Supervisor claimed "Error: Phase 3 incomplete: task was not archived with a terminal status. Revert skipped (user chose to keep changes). Error: repo is dirty; commit/stash your changes before running Ralph." but the task had properly been moved from queue.json to done.json with a status of 'done'. See interaction with an agent below.
---
Phase 3 completion check: action? [1=keep (default), 2=revert, 3=other]: is 'make ci' clean?
[Reasoning] **Confirming CI run with live tests skipped**

Yes, I ran 'make ci SKIP_LIVE_TESTS=1' and all tests passed with live tests skipped as requested.
Yes. 'make ci' completed cleanly with 'SKIP_LIVE_TESTS=1' (live tests were skipped by that flag).

Phase 3 completion check: action? [1=keep (default), 2=revert, 3=other]: the completion file in .ralph/cache/completions is not present for this task ID
[Reasoning] **Checking file presence**
[Command] /bin/zsh -lc 'ls -la /Users/mitchfultz/Projects/splunk-tui/.ralph/cache/completions' (in_progress)
[Command] /bin/zsh -lc 'ls -la /Users/mitchfultz/Projects/splunk-tui/.ralph/cache/completions' (completed) (exit 0)
[Reasoning] **Confirming empty directory and suggesting next steps**

The target directory is empty, likely because the ralph completion file wasn't generated or created. I'll confirm this status and ask if the user wants to regenerate it by rerunning the ralph task, noting it might duplicate work.
Confirmed: '.ralph/cache/completions/' is empty in this repo (no completion file for any task IDs right now).

If you want me to regenerate the completion artifact for 'RQ-0108', say the word and I’ll investigate the right 'ralph' command to do so (without changing task state).

Phase 3 completion check: action? [1=keep (default), 2=revert, 3=other]: what command did your instructions/prompt tell you to do?
Run 'ralph task done --note "<note>" RQ-0108' after completing checks.
Phase 3 completion check: action? [1=keep (default), 2=revert, 3=other]: ^[1
1
[Reasoning] **Requesting clarification on unclear input**
I’m not sure what '' refers to here. Can you clarify what you want me to do or check?

tasks.title: Scan: show validation error details before prompting for post-run action
evidence: User report: when `ralph scan` finishes with an invalid queue, the validation error is only shown after the user selects a post-run action prompt.
User report example shows the error message printing after the prompt line, obscuring the reason until after selection.
plan: Locate the scan post-run validation flow and the prompt that asks how to proceed on validation failure.
Reorder output so validation failure details are printed before prompting for the post-run action.
Ensure raw stdout error file path (if any) is still surfaced alongside the failure details.
Add tests that cover success and failure modes, asserting the error appears before the prompt text.
Update CLI docs/help examples if the user-visible flow changed.
Run `make ci`.
tasks.request:
There is a bug. When 'ralph scan' finishes with an invalid queue the user is not shown the reason until after selecting an option of what to do. See below:
"""
Scan validation failure (post-run): action? [1=keep (default), 2=revert, 3=other]: 1Error: Scan validation failed after run. (raw stdout saved to /var/folders/rf/t1b4c_cn7sgc_f6tkyg0wsk00000gn/T/ralph/ralph_scan_validation_error_bRLFdT/output.txt) Revert skipped (user chose to keep changes).: validate queue set after scan: task[0] created_at must be a valid RFC3339 UTC timestamp (got: 2026-01-27T16:03:39.3NZ, id=RQ-0146). Example: '2026-01-19T05:23:13.000000000Z'.: parse RFC3339 timestamp '2026-01-27T16:03:39.3NZ': the 'offset hour' component could not be parsed: the 'offset hour' component could not be parsed
"""

tasks.title: TUI: standardize confirm dialog keybindings (j/k, arrows, sensible defaults)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/confirm.rs`: `ConfirmRevert` navigation uses Up/Down only (no j/k), unlike other TUI navigation modes.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/render/overlays.rs`: revert dialog instructs “Up/Down: select”, reinforcing the inconsistency.
plan: Standardize confirm dialog navigation keys across confirm modes (arrows + vim keys).
Ensure Enter activates the currently selected option and Esc maps to a clear default.
Update overlay/footer hints to match behavior.
Add tests for confirm revert navigation via both arrows and j/k.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: add “jump to task by ID” command (palette + input)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/events/palette.rs`: `PaletteCommand` enum has no “select task by ID” command.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: app tracks an `id_to_index` map and can rebuild it (`ensure_id_index_map`), which can back a jump-to-ID feature.
`docs/tui-task-management.md`: “Jump-to-ID” is listed as a suggested enhancement but is not implemented.
plan: Add a palette command and an input mode/overlay for entering a task ID (e.g., RQ-####).
Use the id->index cache to select the task and adjust scroll to keep it visible.
Handle missing/invalid IDs with a clear status message.
Add tests for successful jumps and failure modes.
Update `docs/tui-task-management.md` to document the new workflow.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: direct set-status/set-priority commands + optional auto-archive on terminal
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/task_edit.rs`: task status and priority updates are currently “cycle” only (`cycle_status` / `cycle_priority`).
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: archiving terminal tasks is a separate explicit command (`archive_terminal_tasks`).
`docs/tui-task-management.md`: notes the TUI requires a 2-step “set terminal status then archive” workflow and suggests auto-archive / direct set-status as improvements.
plan: Add palette commands for setting an explicit status (Draft/Todo/Doing/Done/Rejected) and priority (Critical/High/Medium/Low).
Implement explicit set operations (not cycling) using the queue edit APIs.
Add an optional prompt (or config toggle) to archive immediately when setting Done/Rejected.
Add tests for explicit set-status/set-priority and the auto-archive/prompt flow.
Update docs and help key reference accordingly.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: improve command palette matching (fuzzy scoring + better ordering)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: `palette_entries` filters commands via case-insensitive substring match only (`title.contains(q)`), with no scoring or ranking.
plan: Introduce a small fuzzy scoring/ranking for palette entries (keeping stable ordering when query is empty).
Sort entries by score and add tie-breakers for predictable results.
Add tests that lock in ranking behavior for common queries.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: add a mode/status bar for discoverability (mode, dirty/save, filters)
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/render/mod.rs`: base UI renders list+details plus a footer, but there is no dedicated header/status bar for current mode or save state.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: app tracks status like `dirty`, `save_error`, and `runner_active`, but most of it is not surfaced persistently in the UI.
plan: Add a compact header/status bar that shows current mode, selection, filters, and save/runner state.
Make the header responsive to terminal width (truncate long fields).
Add rendering tests for small and normal terminal widths.
Run `make ci`.

tasks.title: TUI: handle resize and other non-key events explicitly
evidence: `crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: input loop only processes events matching `Event::Key`; `Event::Resize` is ignored.
`crates/ralph/src/tui/app.rs`: the same pattern implies other event types (mouse/paste) are ignored unless added explicitly.
plan: Handle `Event::Resize` to avoid stale cached layout state and to prevent rendering artifacts on terminal resize.
Ensure non-key events are either ignored intentionally (without side-effects) or handled (mouse support task covers mouse).
Add a focused test (or a small harness) to ensure resize events do not panic and update cached dimensions appropriately.
Run `make ci`.

custom_fields.stale_note: Mouse events now handled (RQ-0324); evidence about mouse being ignored is no longer accurate. Resize/paste still unhandled.
